title: Safeguarding Covered Defense Information and Cyber Incident Reporting
text: Contractors handling Covered Defense Information (CDI) must: implement NIST SP 800-171 security requirements, report cyber incidents to DIBNet within 72 hours, conduct media preservation + damage assessment, identify compromise scope. Applies to all DoD prime + subcontractors with CDI.

title: Notice of NIST SP 800-171 Assessment
text: Contractors must have a current (≤3 years old) Basic, Medium, or High NIST SP 800-171 Assessment posted in the Supplier Performance Risk System (SPRS). Self-assessment for Basic; DCMA / DCSA for Medium / High.

title: NIST SP 800-171 Assessment Requirements
text: Contractors must provide access to facilities + records + cyber personnel during a Government Assessment when required. Must flow down to subs.
Level 1: Foundational. 17 practices aligned to FAR 52.204-21 basic safeguarding. Annual self-assessment. For Federal Contract Information (FCI) only — not for CDI.
Level 2: Advanced. 110 practices aligned to NIST SP 800-171. C3PAO assessment every 3 years for critical CDI; self-assessment otherwise. Mandatory for handling CDI.
Level 3: Expert. 110 NIST SP 800-171 + 24 NIST SP 800-172 practices. DIBCAC assessment every 3 years. Required for the most sensitive CDI.

title: System and Communications Protection
text: Family 3.13: 16 controls covering boundary protection (3.13.1 firewall + intrusion detection), encryption (3.13.8 cryptographic key management + 3.13.11 FIPS-validated crypto), separation of public + private networks (3.13.5), etc.

title: Section 889 of FY2019 NDAA — Prohibition on Covered Telecom
text: Part A (Aug 2019): prohibits Government use of covered telecom equipment / services (Huawei, ZTE, Hytera, Hikvision, Dahua + subsidiaries). Part B (Aug 2020): prohibits Government from contracting with entities that use covered telecom anywhere in their operations. Self-representation in SAM.gov; violations = false claims liability.

title: Counterfeit Electronic Parts — Avoidance, Detection, Mitigation, and Disposition
text: Standard for organizations that purchase / use electronic parts. Risk assessment, supplier management (use of authorized distributors as preferred source), part verification + testing, reporting (GIDEP), control of suspect / confirmed counterfeit parts.

title: Counterfeit Electronic Parts — Avoidance Protocol, Distributors
text: Specifically for independent distributors. Requirements: traceability documentation, visual + electrical inspection, scrap material control, training. Used alongside AS5553.

title: Excluded Parties List System (EPLS)
text: Public list of contractors / individuals barred from receiving Federal contracts due to fraud, false claims, criminal conviction, or administrative debarment. Mandatory pre-award check. Hits = ineligible for award.

title: Buy American Act + Build America Buy America
text: BAA requires Government to acquire domestic end products (>60% domestic content under FAR 25.003 as of 2024, rising to 75% by 2029). BABA (2021) covers federally-funded infrastructure. Exceptions: trade-agreement countries, non-availability, unreasonable cost. Foreign-content disclosure required.

title: Buy American and Balance of Payments Program
text: DoD-specific Buy American implementation. Domestic end product = (a) US-mined, US-produced, OR (b) substantially all of its components are US/qualifying-country components. Qualifying-country list includes ~25 countries with reciprocal defense procurement agreements.

title: ITAR Direct Commercial Sale licenses
text: DDTC license required for export of USML items (defense articles + services + technical data). License types: DSP-5 (permanent), DSP-73 (temporary), TAA (Technical Assistance Agreement), MLA (Manufacturing License Agreement). Foreign-national-employee access to ITAR data inside US = 'deemed export' requiring authorization.

title: Foreign Ownership Control or Influence
text: For cleared defense contractors. FOCI mitigation required when 5%+ foreign ownership, foreign board members, foreign financing, or other foreign influence factors. Mitigation paths: Voting Trust Agreement, Proxy Agreement, Special Security Agreement, Security Control Agreement.
